title: Text Reverser
shortDescription: Reverse text character by character and keep punctuation, spaces, and symbols in the new order.
metaDescription: Reverse any text character by character while preserving spaces, punctuation, and symbols in the output.
intro: Paste text and get the characters reversed immediately.
overview:
This tool reverses the exact characters in your text from end to start. Letters, numbers, spaces, punctuation, and symbols are all moved into reverse order, so the output is useful when you need the text itself flipped rather than reworded.

It works well for short phrases, names, labels, codes, and test strings. If you are checking how a message looks backward or preparing reversed text for design, puzzles, or formatting tests, the result appears instantly on the page.

Because it reverses characters exactly as entered, the tool does not translate, correct, or reorganize your text. If you include line breaks, they are kept as part of the reversed output, which can change the visual layout in a predictable way.
howItWorks: Enter or paste the text you want to reverse.
The tool flips the characters from the last one to the first.
Copy the reversed result and use it wherever you need it.

faq.question: Does this tool reverse words or characters?
faq.answer: It reverses characters. If you type two words, the letters and the space switch order together, so the text reads backward at the character level.

faq.question: What happens to punctuation and numbers?
faq.answer: They are reversed with the rest of the text. A comma, dash, or digit keeps its exact character and moves to a new position in the output.

faq.question: Why does the result look odd with line breaks?
faq.answer: Line breaks are treated as part of the text. When the full string is reversed, the lines can appear in a different visual order than you expect.

faq.question: Can I reverse only part of a sentence?
faq.answer: Yes. Delete the parts you do not want to change, or reverse one selected fragment separately and paste it back into your text.
